layers.title: Abyss hard
layers.method_summary: Seafloor models of rock and gravel developed by Jenkins, C.J. & dbSEABED Contributors (2025) were combined to create a hard bottom proportion. Then hard bottoms were mapped within Abyssal plain depth zone (1000-7500 m) using gebco 2025 data.
layers.known_limitations: The underlying substrate and bathymetry models are uncertain in most areas with a course resolution of about 11km. Direct measurements (e.g. seabed surveys) are required to increase the quality.
layers.source_citation: GEBCO Compilation Group (2025) GEBCO 2025 Grid (doi:10.5285/ 37c52e96-24ea-67ce-e063-7086abc05f29). Available from: https://www.gebco.net/data-products/gridded-bathymetry-data. Jenkins, C.J. & dbSEABED Contributors (2025). Global seafloor sediment texture mappings, 0.1° grid (version 2025-12) [Data set]. HUB Ocean. doi: 10.82510/ab08-yy59 and Jenkins, C.J. & dbSEABED Contributors (2025). Global seafloor rock presence mapping, 0.1° grid (version 2025-12) [Data set]. HUB Ocean. doi:10.82510/5xxh-vs19.

layers.title: Mangrove
layers.method_summary: For this analysis the 2020 (updated 2022) measurements of mangrove coverage from global mangrove watch were used. The GMW mangrove maps are originally derived from a combination of L-band SAR (JERS-1 SAR, ALOS PALSAR and ALOS-2 PALSAR-2) and optical (Landsat) satellite data. Mangrove coverage at 25m resolution was aggregated to proportion per 1km, a gaussian filter at 2km was applied to smoothen transitions. Resampling to final resolution was done using the bilinear sampling algorithm
layers.known_limitations: GMW mangrove maps are derived using mainly radar at 25m resolution. Additional data such as high resolution satellite imagery, land-cover data derived using local knowledge and other relevant data sources could improve results.
layers.source_citation: Bunting, Pete, Ake Rosenqvist, Lammert Hilarides, Richard M. Lucas, Nathan Thomas, Takeo Tadono, Thomas A. Worthington, Mark Spalding, Nicholas J. Murray, and Lisa-Maria Rebelo. 2022. 'Global Mangrove Extent Change 1996?2020: Global Mangrove Watch Version 3.0' Remote Sensing 14, no. 15: 3657.doi.org/10.3390/rs14153657 [accessed 2025 November 12]

layers.title: Shallow hard
layers.method_summary: A seafloor substrate model, developed by C. Jenkins with regional support from S. Fennessy, was used, together with the dbSEABED database. Rock and gravel proportions were combined to create a hard bottom layer. The shallow zone was defined as 0-40 m using GEBCO 2025 data, and yearly average SST (Sea Surface Temperature) from Copernicus data was used to model the tropical zone with a linear ramp-up starting at 23 °C (e.g. trop zone = 0) ending at 25.5 °C (e.g. trop zone =1).
layers.known_limitations: The underlying substrate and bathymetry models are uncertain in most areas of the region.
layers.source_citation:
European Union-Copernicus Marine Service. Global Ocean Physics Reanalysis

The shelf zone was defined as 40-200 m using GEBCO 2025 data, and yearly average SST (Sea Surface Temperature) from Copernicus data was used to model the tropical zone with a linear ramp-up starting at 23 °C (e.g. trop zone = 0) ending at 25.5 °C (e.g. trop zone =1).

layers.title: Thermal vents
layers.description: Formations built around active hydrothermal vents

layers.title: Air pollution
layers.description: Airborne pullutants (NOx ,SOx, etc.) from activities on land or sea

layers.title: Artisanal fishing
layers.description: Small-scale fisheries using nets, cages, spears

layers.title: Boating
layers.method_summary: Recreational boating was estimated using global AIS data 2024 provided by Global fishing watch. The data is summarized in hours / year spent by passenger boats at a given position at a 50 arc second resolution (1111m).
layers.known_limitations: The estimation does not take into account the size of the passenger boat which could be a cruise liner or ferry. Assuming a larger boat has a higher impact
layers.source_citation: Global Fishing Watch. [2024], updated daily. [AIS Vessel Presence, Version 3]

layers.title: Crustacean pots
layers.description: Commercial fishing of lobsters, crabs, shrimps using pots, cages, nets

layers.title: Demersal gillnet
layers.description: Commercial fishing using gillnets close to the bottom
layers.known_limitations: The Global Fishing Watch data is based on advanced algorithms to estimate fishing effort. However, there is a risk that vessels are incorrectly identified as fishing in the algorithm. Further, these data may not identify unregistered fishing boats.
layers.source_citation: Global Fishing Watch. [2025], updated daily. [Global Fishing Effort, Version 3]

layers.title: Demersal trawl
layers.known_limitations: The Global Fishing Watch data is based on advanced algorithms to estimate fishing effort. However, there is a risk that vessels are incorrectly identified as fishing in the algorithm. Further, these data may not identify unregistered fishing boats.
layers.source_citation: Global Fishing Watch. [2025], updated daily. [Global Fishing Effort, Version 3]

layers.title: Diving
layers.description: Diving with and without scuba gear

layers.title: Floating longline
layers.description: Pelagic fishing with long-line baited hooks (floating)
layers.known_limitations: The Global Fishing Watch data is based on advanced algorithms to estimate fishing effort. However, there is a risk that vessels are incorrectly identified as fishing in the algorithm. Further, these data may not identify unregistered fishing boats.

layers.title: Hydrology change
layers.description: Major hydrological changes, e.g. impaired currents or water exchange

layers.title: Industrial pollution
layers.description: Operational discharge of industrial waste or cooling water

layers.title: Inorganic toxins
layers.description: High concentrations of inorganic toxins (e.g. heavy metals) in inflow and sediment

layers.title: Invasive species
layers.description: Non-native species spreading in a new environment at the expense of native species

layers.title: Land reclamation
layers.description: Filling of wetland or inter-tidal areas to create more land

layers.title: Light pollution
layers.description: Artificial lights from buildings, settlements and platforms (not fishing)

layers.title: Oil gas industry
layers.description: Oil and gas infrastructure, operational spills, service shipping

layers.title: Oilspill risk
layers.description: Accidental spill of oil from ship or oil rig (often crude oil)

layers.title: Organic toxins
layers.description: High concentrations of organic toxins (PCB, DDT, dioxin, etc.) in inflow and sediment

layers.title: Other fishing
layers.description: Unreported fishing detected by nighlight satellite

layers.title: Pelagic gillnet
layers.description: Commercial fishing using gillnets floating close to the surface

layers.title: Pelagic seine
layers.description: Commercial purse seine for open water (e.g. catching mackerel, tuna)

layers.title: Plastic litter
layers.description: Macroplastic pollution from waste (plastic objects >5 mm), projected 2025
layers.providers: Kaandorp MLA, Lobelle D, Kehl C, Dijkstra HA, van Sebille E
layers.method_summary: A new global model of plastic pollution in the oceans and coastline, available as 60-km hexagon grids, was adjusted to 250m, 500m or 1km grid. Data was available for a range of particle sizes, as particle mass or concentration and with predictions from years 2015, 2020 and 2025. For the WIO Symphony model, total concentration of particles larger than 5 mm from the 2020 prediction was used. The data was split up in two ways, the shore prediction, and the pelagic data (divided into 0-5 m (surface), 5-50 m, 50-500 m and 500 m-inf depth layers). The interpolation for each individual layer was made using the centre points of the hexagon grids, applying Inverse Distance Weighting. Shoreline plastic: The interpolated plastic shore data model was multiplied with shoreline proportion per 1-km, hence assuming more complex shorelines result in a higher pressure from plastic pollution within a 1-km area. In addition, a 2-km Gaussian filter was run and combined with this data, assuming plastic has a local spread into the nearby environment. Finally, the data was normalized to 0-100 using the maximum value. Pelagic plastic: The interpolated concentrations of plastic particles for each depth layer where combined using the average value in each cell. The data were then normalized to 0-100 using the global maximum value. Combined product: The two normalized layers were combined into one using the maximum value in each cell.
layers.known_limitations: The plastic layer is limited by the resolution and accuracy of a global model. To better represent the coastal area and trace the local sources of plastic pollution, additional data such as high resolution, satellite imagery, land-cover data, census data, and other relevant data sources would be needed.
layers.source_citation: Kaandorp MLA, Lobelle D, Kehl C, Dijkstra HA, van Sebille E. The global marine plastic mass budget is dominated by large long-lived objects [dataset]. 2023 [accessed 2025 November 12]. In press, Supplementary Material.

layers.title: Ports
layers.description: Hard infrastructure, loss of beach, spills, turbidity

layers.title: Seabed mining
layers.description: Extraction of seabed minerals (not dredging)/beach mining

layers.title: Seismic surveys
layers.description: Prospecting of seabed resources using impulsive sonars

layers.title: Ship pollution
layers.method_summary: Ship pollution was estimated using global AIS data 2024 provided by Global fishing watch. The data is summarized in hours / year spent by any vessel type at a given position at a 50 arc second resolution (1111m).
layers.known_limitations: Calculations are not taking ship speed into account. Also the estimation does not take into account the proportion of the different vessel types that may be overrepresented in certain areas, assuming larger vessels pollute more.
layers.source_citation: Global Fishing Watch. [2024], updated daily. [AIS Vessel Presence, Version 3],

layers.title: Ship strike
layers.description: Potential collision between ship and animal, when ships pass through area

layers.title: Submarine cables
layers.description: Electromagnetic fields from power cables or communication lines

layers.title: Temperature rise
layers.description: Temperature rise, incl. heat waves, caused by climate change

layers.title: Weighted longline
layers.description: Demersal fishing with long-line baited hooks (weighted down to bottom)
